# CPU-only inference dependencies for the Raspberry Pi 5 deployment target.
# Loosely version-pinned (>=, tested-known-good floor) rather than exact-pinned, since exact PC
# pins (requirements-dev.txt) may not have prebuilt aarch64 wheels on a given date.
#
# After `scripts/setup_pi.sh` succeeds on real hardware, `requirements-pi-lock.txt` (generated by
# `pip freeze`) becomes the authoritative record of what was actually installed — treat this file
# as the hand-authored starting point, not the source of truth once a lock file exists.
#
# Deliberately EXCLUDED, flagged instead of silently dropped:
#   - apex: only imported by Waveglow's training scripts (Waveglow/train.py,
#     Waveglow/tacotron2/train.py) and Waveglow/inference.py. Waveglow is not part of the active
#     pipeline (commented out under vocoder_models in config_tts.yaml; default vocoder is
#     hifi-gan-master). Also the exact package that mis-resolves on PyPI (the reason for this
#     split) and CUDA-only (Waveglow's `amp` usage), so it would very likely fail to build on
#     aarch64 CPU-only even if it were needed. If Waveglow support is ever required on the Pi,
#     revisit this — do not add apex back silently.
#   - tensorflow / tensor2tensor / tgt / tensorboardX / pyworld / scikit_learn / librosa (explicit
#     pin): FastSpeech2 *training/preprocessing*-only deps, not needed to run synthesize.py against
#     an already-trained checkpoint. librosa is still present transitively (noisereduce depends on
#     it), just not pinned here.
#   - simpleaudio / sounddevice: both are imported only under `if platform.system() == "Windows":`
#     in audio_utils.py and gui_utils.py — dead code on Linux. Playback on the Pi goes through
#     pydub.playback.play(), which shells out to `ffplay` (see apt-packages-pi.txt: ffmpeg).
torch>=2.4.1
PyYAML>=6.0.2
numpy>=2.0.2
unicode>=2.9
unidecode>=1.3.8
inflect>=7.4.0
regex>=2024.9.11
scipy>=1.14.1
matplotlib>=3.9.2
transformers>=4.45.1
noisereduce>=3.0.2
pydub>=0.25.1
g2p_en>=2.1.0
pypinyin>=0.53.0
sacremoses>=0.1.1
